pr.title: Link to decomp.dev instead of repo on Wiki TU page
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the TU wiki generation script so file links point to the corresponding decomp.dev unit page instead of GitHub source blobs. The change centralizes file/link generation in a new helper, URL-encodes unit names for the decomp.dev query parameter, and includes minor Python typing cleanup.
what_changed: Replaced generated Markdown links from `../blob/master/src/{file}.c` with `https://decomp.dev/doldecomp/melee?unit=...` links.
Added `urllib.parse.quote` and uses `quote(unit['name'], safe='')` so TU names are safe inside the decomp.dev `unit` query parameter.
Removed the separate `file_of(unit)` helper and introduced `file_link(unit) -> tuple[str, str]`, returning both the stripped display file path and the Markdown link.
Updated both full and slim wiki rows to use the new decomp.dev link helper.
Modernized type annotations from `Dict[...]` and `Optional[...]` to built-in generics and union syntax such as `dict[str, dict[str, str]]`, `Path | None`, and `float | None`.
smart_moves: Centralized URL construction in `file_link`, avoiding duplicated link formatting between `row_full` and `row_slim`.
Kept the existing display text behavior by continuing to strip the leading module component, while changing only the link target.
Used URL quoting for the raw unit name instead of interpolating it directly into the query string, which protects paths containing `/` or other special characters.
Preserved assignee lookup behavior by returning the stripped file path separately from the generated link.
decomp_lessons: For progress/wiki tooling, linking directly to decomp.dev can be more useful than linking to repository source files because it takes users to the matching/decompilation view for the exact translation unit.
When generating TU links, keep a distinction between the user-facing file path and the canonical unit identifier used by tooling.
If a script emits the same link format in multiple table variants, factor that formatting into one helper so future URL scheme changes only need one edit.
Always URL-encode translation unit identifiers when placing them in query parameters.
naming_conventions: `file_link(unit)` returns `(file, link)`, where `file` is the stripped display/lookup path and `link` is the Markdown-formatted decomp.dev link.
The generated URL uses the decomp.dev project path `doldecomp/melee` and a `unit` query parameter containing the original `unit['name']` value.
The display path continues to strip the leading module component such as `main/` before showing the file in wiki tables.
follow_up_queries: Where else do generated docs or wiki pages link to GitHub source blobs instead of decomp.dev units?
Do all decomp.dev unit links in project tooling URL-encode the unit name with `quote(..., safe='')`?
Are there other duplicated Markdown link helpers in `tools/wiki_tu.py` or related wiki generation scripts?
